title: neonauclea celebica
intro: Neonauclea celebica, also known as Celebica or Philippine medinilla, is a plant species endemic to the Philippines, characterized by its ornamental and medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. Neonauclea celebica is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
